to summarize

Aequor: get tentacles that do follow up attacks at turn end, there's three varieties that also make shields or do more damage at the cost of one tentacle 

Caro: fill Embryo gauge to get Embryo cards - said cards can either be played to charge Ults, or be Devoured when casting an Ult for additional effects; there's also Crimson Furnace gauge that restores your HP when emptied

Ultra: first character card played each turn gets stored, and generally, after 5 cards are stored, you get an additional turn that doesn't increase the turn count; Ultra characters' cards tend to have special Leap/Quasar effects that activate if that card is the first one played in that turn (or if played during the additional turn at any point)

Chaos: mainly included to boost the other realms' effects, a full Chaos team mainly interacts with Posses and has boosted Keyflare generation 

(as far as I'm personally aware, since I haven't played a full Chaos team enough to be sure)
(also Embryo thing is just ✨ aesthetics ✨ , none of the characters there actually eat babies in lore)

# Halfsies CR & CD:

Assuming a 100% covenant set which gets half of the substats dedicated to CR
and the other half to CD. And the mainstats are:  CR > CD we have the following stats:

- substats CR:  14.4 
- main CR : 12
- main DR: 28%
- total CR: 30.6

- substats CD: 21.6
- main CD: 0
- total CD: 25.8

# Full DR
 Full DR, always choose DR > CR on main stats
- substats DR: 100,8
- main DR: 42
- total DR: 142.8

- main CR: 8%
- total CR:  29.42
- total CD: 21.42

So, the tldr is: as cheri mentioned, if you are rich enough and can afford a full DR set, go bananas, but beware you'll not notice the difference between the sets until you hit DR trigger
But still nice if you are planning to max out crit rate
